items.title: Taiwan drone exports to Europe soar 40-fold as Ukraine war fuels demand
Approvato il 20esimo pacchetto di sanzioni Ue alla Russia
items.description: Lo fa sapere la presidenza di turno cipriota

items.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran says it has seized two ships in strait of Hormuz after Trump extends ceasefire
items.description: State media says the ships have been transferred to Iran’s coastTrump announces ceasefire extensionWhy Trump’s erratic commentary is the real block to an Iran dealIf you’re just joining us, here’s the main news of the day. It is 9.30am in Tehran, 9am in Jerusalem and Beirut, and 2am in Washington DC.Donald Trump unilat…

items.title: EU unblocks €90 billion loan to Ukraine
items.description: Envoys from EU member states have finally paved the way for a €90 billion loan to Ukraine that had long been blocked by Hungary. They also approved a 20th package of sanctions on Russia over its invasion of its neighbor.

items.title: In south Lebanon village, Hezbollah buries fighters killed in war with Israel
items.description: Hezbollah held a mass funeral in south Lebanon's Kfar Sir on Tuesday for more than a dozen fighters killed during more than six weeks of war with Israel. The ceremony came after Israel and Lebanon entered into a 10-day ceasefire on Friday announced by US President Donald Trump. Iran-backed Hezbollah has not provided th…

items.title: Settler arson attack reportedly wounds 8 Palestinians, including baby; police launch probe
items.description: IDF says troops arrived in Nablus-area village but found no suspects, following reports that house and cars were set on fire, amid failure to stem wave of West Bank attacks The post Settler arson attack reportedly wounds 8 Palestinians, including baby; police launch probe appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: Ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz after ceasefire extension
items.description: Three vessels came under fire in the Strait of Hormuz, just hours after the U.S. and Iran failed to meet in Pakistan for talks to end the war and as President Trump indefinitely extended the ceasefire.

items.title: Gaza’s unseen casualties: A surge in stillbirths and birth defects
items.description: Gaza sees a 140% rise in stillbirths and a doubling of congenital anomalies as the devastating impacts of war linger.

items.title: Woman and Child Killed in Ukrainian Drone Strike on Samara Region
items.description: Ukraine previously attacked an oil refinery in the city of Syzran, where a woman and a child were killed early Wednesday.

items.title: On Ukraine's 'Road of Life,' Drones Hunt Targets Near The Front Line
items.description: Ukrainian soldiers patrol near Druzhkivka as Russian drone and air strikes intensify. Along the "Road of Life" to Kostyantynivka -- a vital but deadly route -- soldiers hunt drones while civilians who remain say they have nowhere else to go.

items.title: Israeli soldiers punished over vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
items.description: Two soldiers will be removed from combat duty and receive 30 days of military detention, Israel's military says.

items.title: Israeli films caught between calls for boycott and government threats
items.description: Their access to international festivals and co-productions has become increasingly complicated as Israel's image has deteriorated since the war of destruction in Gaza that followed the October 7 terrorist attack. The culture minister, for his part, is threatening to cut off state funding.

items.title: EXCLUSIVE: Are Robots Replacing Soldiers in Ukraine War? What’s Really Happening on the Front Line
items.description: Ukrainian troops say ground robots are rapidly expanding on the front line to deliver supplies and evacuate wounded, reducing risks for soldiers but not replacing them.

items.title: Millions of desperate Sudanese return home amid dire conditions as war rages
items.description: Three years into the devastating conflict in Sudan, nearly four million displaced people have returned to their places of origin across the country, only to face “another struggle for survival”, the UN International Organization for Migration (IOM) said on Tuesday.

items.title: Amid ceasefire in Lebanon, a rush home – if it’s reachable and still there
items.description: As civilians displaced by the Israel-Hezbollah conflict in Lebanon try to return to their war-damaged homes, a new Israeli occupation zone in the country’s south poses an obstacle for many. Meanwhile, the ceasefire has reduced but not eliminated the fighting.
